---
title: "Clean and Merge"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Clean and Merge}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

```{r}
library(MadagascarWeatherStations)
```

<!-- WARNING - This vignette is generated by {fusen} from dev/flat_clean_and_merge.Rmd: do not edit by hand -->




This notebook develops a simple function that is the main data processing function in the pipeline. 
It takes raw data from the "data/" directory, cleans it, and merges it into a single dataset 
that can be used for analysis and visualization.

First, we must check that all of the files we receive are uniform and consistent.

```{r}
library(targets)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(janitor)
library(here)
```


Reading in the data from the targets pipeline:
```{r}
raw_data_files <- tar_read(raw_data_files, store = here("_targets"))

raw_data_files
```


We can test out reading in one of these files:
```{r}
dat <- read_csv(raw_data_files[[1]])
dat
```


This looks great. Let's clean up the column names:
```{r}
dat <- dat %>%
  clean_names()
```


It looks like the "date" and "simple date" columns
are being read in differently, based on local time.

We should rename that column to local_date to avoid confusion:
```{r}
dat <- dat %>%
  rename(local_date = simple_date)
```


This will do for now, but we should eventually add
more tests for when future data are added.

# clean_and_merge
